cis: symbol. The function c i s (x) = cos ⁡ x + i sin ⁡ x {\displaystyle \mathrm {cis} (x)=\cos x+i\sin x}.
epic: noun. An extended narrative poem in elevated or dignified language, celebrating the feats of a deity, demigod (heroic epic), other legend
fice: noun. Alternative form of feist (“a feist dog”).
fie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fyer.
fisc: noun. The public treasury of Rome.
ice: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Icelandic.
pec: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Pesisir.
pes: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iranian Persian.
pic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pinji.
pice: noun. Alternative form of paisa, especially in the form of coins worth 20 cash.
pie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Piro.
pis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pijin.
psi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeast Pashayi.
sec: symbol. Symbol of the trigonometric function secant.
sei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seri.
seif: noun. A sand dune that elongates parallel to the prevailing wind.
sepic: adjective. Of or pertaining to sepia; done in sepia.
sic: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Malinguat.
sip: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sikkimese.
sipe: noun. Slit in a tire to drain away surface water and improve traction.
spic: noun. Synonym of Latino, an inhabitant of Latin America or person of Latin American descent.
spice: noun. Aromatic or pungent plant matter (usually dried) used to season or flavor food.
cpi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chinese Pidgin English.
epi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Epie.
fcs: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Quebec Sign Language.
feis: noun. An Irish festival, usually including folk music, dancing, and sports.
fies: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of fy
fip: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fipa.
pcf: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Paliyan.
pci: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Duruwa.
scf: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for San Miguel Creole French.
sicc: adjective. Eye dialect spelling of sick (“in poor health; ill”).
sie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Simaa.
sif: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siamou.
